filesCreated.purpose: Block-specific CSS for Hero gradient styling using generic --gradient-left/--gradient-right variables

filesModified.purpose: Removed ALL hero-specific gradient code (lines 143-206: .hero custom properties, per-color overrides, .top-gradient @apply rule). Generic gradient system (--gradient-left/--gradient-right) remains.
Added overflow-y: clip to .wp-site-blocks to prevent absolutely positioned gradients from extending page beyond content

testPage.spacerPlacement: Inside each DemoContainer after hero block, except the last DemoContainer

warnings: Asset size limit warnings (340 KiB editor.js)

syncStatus.details: No TSX/PHP changes required. CSS architecture changes only.

heroCss.content: .top-gradient with position: absolute, height: 150vw, z-index: -10, 3-layer background using --accent-color, --gradient-left, --gradient-right
heroCss.eliminatedCode: All 6 per-color .hero.X overrides (neon-green, blue, green, yellow, orange, purple). The generic gradient system in colors.css already handles per-color switching.

bodyCss.rationale: Clips absolutely positioned elements at page content boundary. Prevents gradient from growing document past footer. Does not create scroll container, does not affect fixed/sticky elements, does not create new block formatting context.

postHeroVerification.sharedClass: PostHero uses class='post-hero hero ...' on its <section> element, so it inherits .top-gradient rules from Hero.css via the 'hero' class
postHeroVerification.fileChangesNeeded: None - PostHero automatically benefits from Hero.css and overflow-y:clip on .wp-site-blocks

overflowFix.approach: Added overflow-y: clip to .wp-site-blocks wrapper in body.css
overflowFix.preservedBehavior: Hero section keeps overflow-visible for gradient bleed effect. Gradient bleeds freely between blocks within .wp-site-blocks.
overflowFix.eliminatedIssue: Gradient no longer extends document scrollable area past footer
